title: Micron (MU) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Micron delivered record quarterly results with $23.9 billion in revenue, up 196% year-over-year. The company achieved a 75% gross margin and generated $6.9 billion in free cash flow. Management emphasized that memory makes AI smarter and more capable, positioning memory as a strategic asset in the AI era. Financial Highlights: DRAM revenue reached $18.8 billion (79% of total), NAND revenue totaled $5.0 billion (21% of total), Operating margin expanded to 69%, Net cash position hit a company record of $6.5 billion, Board approved a 30% dividend increase to $0.15 per share. Strategic Developments: Management secured the company's first five-year Strategic Customer Agreement (SCA), representing a shift from traditional one-year Long-Term Agreements. The SCA provides multiyear volume and supply commitments for enhanced visibility and stability. Market Conditions: Leadership noted that some key customers can only be fulfilled 50% to two-thirds of their demand, highlighting persistent supply constraints. AI demand is expected to drive data center memory to exceed 50% of industry TAM in calendar 2026 for the first time. Forward Guidance: Q3 revenue projected at $33.5 billion with 81% gross margin—surpassing any prior year's full revenue.
